satire_attempt: Engineering Manager Realizes 'Symmetry of Dread' Is The Real KPI. After three architecture reviews and a six-page Notion doc, local manager bravely escalates to the VP: 'I cannot recommend either path — the frame is the bug.' Sources confirm the manager will be celebrated in next quarter's all-hands as a master of 'epistemic exhaustion,' a buzzword the company plans to add to the engineering ladder rubric.
verdict_reason: The satire only lands if you ignore the counter-example, which the verse foregrounds: 'Engineers who use 2.6 as cover for refusing to make 50/50 calls have inverted it. Real equipoise commits.' To mock the verse you have to disagree with it on its merits — i.e. argue that the symmetric-dread diagnostic is fake — and that argument has to engage the falsifiability check. PASS.
